input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. This is a code generation/guidance request - I don't need to explore any codebase. Let me create the files directly.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout menu with:
   - "Dashboard" section (single page)
   - "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
   - "Settings" page accessible from flyout
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's how it works:

**Flyout structure:**

| Flyout Item | Content |
|---|---|
| Dashboard | Single `ShellContent` page |
| Products | Two `Tab`s — "Active" and "Archived" — shown as bottom tabs when selected |
| Settings | Single `ShellContent` page |

**Key points:**

- Each `FlyoutItem` becomes a menu entry. The Products item wraps two `<Tab>` elements, which render as bottom tabs on mobile.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until the user navigates there.
- `ProductDetailsPage` is registered via `Routing.RegisterRoute` in the code-behind because it's a *detail* page (not in the shell visual tree). Navigate to it from a product list like:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

You'll need to create the five page classes under a `Pages/` folder: `DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, and `ProductDetailsPage`.
